title: Implement Daily Digest feature
description: docs/00-spec.md describes a Daily Digest as a primary use-case: 'Every day, send the user a digest with relevant information, such as appointments for the day, task(s) that require attention, maybe the weather forecast.' No code currently exists. Approach: leverage the existing scheduler module to register a recurring cron job that (1) queries db/tasks for pending high-priority items, (2) calls calendar MCP (Fastmail CalDav already configured) for today's events, (3) optionally pulls a weather summary, (4) DMs the owner. Should be configurable via config/default.toml (digest time, included sections).

title: Commit and push in-flight schedule feature changes
description: Uncommitted changes add ~196 lines across src/bot/commands/schedule.rs, src/db/schedules.rs, src/models/schedule.rs (test additions per survey), plus an AGENTS.md tweak. Two new untracked workflow files (.github/workflows/ci.yml, release.yml) also need to be committed. Branch 'main' is 1 commit ahead of origin/main and needs to be pushed. Run cargo test + cargo clippy before commit; group commits logically (schedule tests; CI workflows; AGENTS.md).

title: Add tests for MCP manager and client
description: src/mcp/mod.rs (McpManager) and src/mcp/client.rs (McpClient) have no tests. Cover: (1) tool name namespacing (server.tool), (2) routing a call_tool() to the correct McpClient by namespace, (3) listing tools across multiple servers, (4) error handling when a server is down or returns an error. Use a fake stdio MCP transport or a dummy in-process server for the McpClient tests.

title: Add integration test for scheduler trigger to Discord DM flow
description: src/scheduler/mod.rs is untested end-to-end. Add an integration test that: (1) registers a one-off cron job that fires within ~2 seconds, (2) verifies the job runs and produces the expected DM payload (mock the Discord http or use a trait seam around DM sending so the test can capture the message), (3) covers add/remove of jobs at runtime mapping schedule DB id -> cron job UUID. This closes the largest behavioral gap noted in the schedule feature survey.

title: Add tests for LLM provider implementations
description: src/llm/{claude,openai,ollama}.rs have zero unit tests. Add tests using a mocked HTTP layer (e.g. wiremock or a hand-rolled reqwest::Client with a custom transport) to cover: (1) request body construction (messages, tools, system prompt), (2) response parsing for plain text replies, (3) response parsing for tool_use replies with multiple tool calls, (4) error paths (rate limit, malformed JSON, network error). Schedules and tasks layers already have ~13 tests and are well-covered; LLM providers are the largest untested surface area.

title: Integrate GitHub MCP server
description: docs/00-spec.md lists a GitHub MCP server as a planned integration: 'A Github MCP that provides a way to interact with a Github account.' Not configured in config/mcp_servers.toml. Decide whether to use the official github-mcp-server over stdio or a community alternative, add it to config/mcp_servers.toml with appropriate auth (PAT via env var), and verify the /ask command can route GitHub queries through it.

title: Add tests for Discord command handlers (ask, tools, ping)
description: src/bot/commands/{ask,tools,ping}.rs have no tests. ping is trivial and may not need any. ask is the most complex (LLM <-> MCP tool-use loop, max 10 iterations) and the highest-value target: add a test that runs the loop with a stub LlmProvider and stub McpManager to verify (1) tool calls are dispatched, (2) tool results are fed back, (3) the iteration cap is enforced, (4) final text is returned. tools.rs lists tools from McpManager and can share fixtures with the MCP tests.

title: Resolve SearXNG MCP server: host or remove
description: config/mcp_servers.toml:8 has a TODO comment about the SearXNG search MCP: 'Need to figure out where/how to host this, or to use the stdio cli.' Decision needed: (a) self-host SearXNG instance and a SearXNG-MCP bridge, (b) use the stdio CLI variant if one exists, or (c) drop the entry until a path forward is chosen. Currently the entry is commented out so it has no functional impact, but the TODO blocks completing the search/research story.
